english: octangular pillar Stg., (v. zúr-čan (zur can) and zúl-ma (zul ma) below); zur bźi (zur bzhi) the four corners Sch. — 3. side, *zúr-du (*zur du) (or lóg-su (logs su)) z̀ag-pa* (zhag pa) C. to lay aside; zúr-(du) bkól-ba (zur du bkol ba) Lex., Sch.: to lay up, put by, spare, save; zúr-du °krid-pa (zur du 'khrid pa) to take aside, apart, for a private conversation; so also zur p̌yín-pa (zur phyin pa) Stg.; zúr-du (zur du), zúr-gyis (zur gyis) B., *zúr-na* (*zur na) W., indirectly, by the way, by the by, incidentally, zúr-du smrá-ba (zur du smra ba) to speak indirectly, by hints Cs.; zúr-gyis mtsón-pa (zur gyis mtshon pa) Tar. to note, point out only by hints or insinuations Schf.; hence perh. tsig zúr (tshig zur) invective speech, *tsig-zúr ma zer* (*tshig zur ma zer) W. no invectives! don't be personal! zur zá-ba (zur za ba) is prob. the same, where Sch. has: to address harshly; *zúr-ne láb-če* (*zur nas lab ce) W. to learn or study privately (out of school-time, or, not with the appointed master); zur bz̀ugs-pa (zur bzhugs pa) Cs. (prob. for zúr-du (zur du)) to lead a private life (cf. zúr-pa (zur pa)); zur mig ltá-ba (zur mig lta ba) to look sideways, askance, to leer, squint Sch. — 4. outline, k̇yod daṅ zur °dra tsam yaṅ sa steṅ med (khyod dang zur 'dra tsam yang sa steng med) none on earth is like you, or can be compared to you, even in a general outline Pth.; °di-dag zur tsam bsdú-ba yín-gyis ('di dag zur tsam bsdu ba yin gyis) this is merely a brief outline, extract, sketch Glr. and elsewh., frq., also zur tsam yín-gyis (zur tsam yin gyis) Glr. — Comp. zur-bkód (zur bkod), zúr-°débs (zur 'debs), Sch.: 'founded for a special purpose'. — zúr-čan (zur can) cornered, angular, yí-ge (yi ge) Glr. p. 31, a sort of type or printing-letter, = klui yí-ge (klu'i yi ge), v. also no. 2 above. — zur-čág (zur chag) Sch.: prop., having a broken edge, damaged by being knocked about; gen. fig., of words and grammatical forms: faulty, corrupted, misapplied; Liś. and elsewh., Ssk. अपभ्रंश. the most vitiated Prakrit-dialect Was. (267). — zur-°débs (zur 'debs) = zur-bkód-zur-nór (zur bkod zur nor) private goods Cs. — zur-pa (zur pa) one out of office, a private individual Cs. zúr-ma (zur ma) = zur (zur) prov. — zur-ysós (zur gsos) educated by strangers Sch.
W. cornered, angular, = zúr-čan (zur can); *p̌e*-zúl* (*phe' zul) lotus-edged, of bowls, dishes, plates, that are of a polygonal or radiated shape.

I. num. figure: 112. II., also zé-ba (ze ba) B., W., zeu (ze'u) Cs. 1. hump of a camel, zebu etc. Cs. — 2. crest, of birds, dragons etc. Glr., S.g.; also ze-próg (ze prog) Lex. — zé-k̇a (ze kha) Cs.: 1. 'hump. 2. decorated pad or cushion'. — ze-rṅóg (ze rngog) Cs. = zé-ba (ze ba). — ze-°brú (ze 'bru), zeu-°brú (ze'u 'bru) Glr., Mṅg. the anthers of a flower.
W. the maw or fourth stomach of ruminating animals.
saltpetre S.g.; zé-tswa-čan (ze tshwa can) containing saltpetre, nitrous; zé-tsai skyúr-rtsi (ze tshwa'i skyur rtsi) nitric acid Cs.
tú-ba yyás-zeṅ yyón-zeṅ byás-pa (thu ba g.yas zeng g.yon zeng byas pa) the skirts of the coat on the right and left side folded back, tucked up Mil.

I. sbst. 1. brush, p̌ag-zéd (phag zed) brush of hog's bristles; byab-zéd (byab zed) clothes-brush, dust-brush Cs.; so-zéd (so zed) tooth-brush Cs. — 2. edge C. — II. adj Sch.: 'broken off, damaged, injured; zéd-laṅs (zed langs) chink, crack, rent; zéd-°dug-pa (zed 'dug pa) to crumble at the top' (?)

1. cask, barrel, tun, often sonsisting merely of an excavated piece of a willow-tree, the Tibetans knowing but little of coopery C., W. — 2. box, chest W. — zem-śiṅ (zem shing) the body or wood of a vessel, zem-mtíl (zem mthil) the bottom of a vessel Cs.
1. v. yzer (gzer). — 2. taik, cf. brjod (brjod). — 3. n of a small animal Med.

1. (seldom °dzér-ba ('dzer ba)) to say, esp. later literat. and vulg; k̇yod zér-ba bdén-no (khyod zer ba bden no) you say rightly Dzl. (where at other times always smra-ba (smra ba) is used inst. of it); he he zer bgád-pas (he he zer bgad pas) they laughed he, he! Glr.; čos dar zer rgyai yig-tsaṅ-na °dug (chos dar zer rgya'i yig tshang na 'dug) then the doctrine was diffused, say the Chinese records Glr.; after words quoted: ... zér-bar °dúg-pas (... zer bar 'dug pas) thus having been spoken, read, heard Glr.; 'yin' zer bsnyon byás-so ('yin' zer bsnyon byas so) saying 'it is he', she told a lie Glr., and so frq. zer (zer), where in earlier literat. z̀es (zhes) is used; zér-na (zer na) 1. if one says, esp. for the older z̀e- (zhe-)
